mission.title: Install and Configure K8gb on Kubernetes
mission.description: K8GB is a cloud-native Kubernetes Global Balancer that provides a solution for global service load balancing across multiple clusters. Installing K8GB allows you to manage traffic efficiently and ensure high availability for your applications.

steps.title: Add the K8GB Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the K8GB Helm chart repository to your Helm client to access the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo add k8gb https://www.k8gb.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install K8GB using Helm
steps.description:
Install K8GB in a new namespace called 'k8gb' with the specified version.
```bash
helm install k8gb k8gb/k8gb --namespace k8gb --create-namespace --version v0.18.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the K8GB pods are running successfully in the 'k8gb' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n k8gb
```

steps.title: Set up a Global Load Balancer
steps.description:
Create a sample GSLB resource to manage your services globally. Note: K8gb requires a custom values.yaml with your DNS zone, edge DNS servers, and cluster geo tags configured.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: k8gb.absa.oss/v1beta1
kind: Gslb
metadata:
  name: hello-world-gslb
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    k8gb.io/hostname: "hello.lb.mycompany.com"
spec:
  resourceRef:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    matchLabels:
      app: hello-world
  strategy:
    type: failover
    primaryGeoTag: "eu"
    dnsTtlSeconds: 30
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of K8GB will show the K8GB pods running in the 'k8gb' namespace. You can also verify the GSLB resource is created and configured correctly by checking the status of your services.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add k8gb https://www.k8gb.io
helm repo update
helm install k8gb k8gb/k8gb --namespace k8gb --create-namespace --version v0.18.1

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: k8gb.absa.oss/v1beta1
kind: Gslb
metadata:
  name: hello-world-gslb
  namespace: default
  annotations:
    k8gb.io/hostname: "hello.lb.mycompany.com"
spec:
  resourceRef:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Service
    matchLabels:
      app: hello-world
  strategy:
    type: failover
    primaryGeoTag: "eu"
    dnsTtlSeconds: 30
  splitBrainThresholdSeconds: 300
EOF

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the K8GB release:
```bash
helm uninstall k8gb -n k8gb
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the CRDs and namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete crd gslbs.k8gb.absa.oss dnsendpoints.externaldns.k8s.io
kubectl delete namespace k8gb
```

upgrade.title: Backup and upgrade
upgrade.description:
Back up current values and upgrade:
```bash
helm get values k8gb -n k8gb > k8gb-backup.yaml
helm repo update
helm upgrade k8gb k8gb/k8gb -n k8gb -f k8gb-backup.yaml
kubectl get pods -n k8gb
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This issue indicates that the K8GB pods are failing to start. Check the logs for more information using the following command:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n k8gb
```

troubleshooting.title: GSLB resource not resolving
troubleshooting.description:
If the GSLB resource is not resolving, ensure that the annotations are correctly set and the service it references is running:
```bash
kubectl get gslb -n default
kubectl get svc -n default
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm upgrade fails with version conflict
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a version conflict during the upgrade, check the current version of K8GB installed:
```bash
helm list -n k8gb
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources allocated
troubleshooting.description:
If K8GB is not performing as expected, verify that the resource limits are appropriately set. Check the deployment configuration:
```bash
kubectl get deployment k8gb -n k8gb -o yaml
```

prerequisites.description: Requires an Ingress Controller (NGINX, Istio, or AWS ALB), an EdgeDNS provider (Route53, Cloudflare, Infoblox, NS1, etc.), and a custom values.yaml with DNS zone and cluster geo tag configuration.
